summary: Stunning soundboard capture of the Fillmore West ’69 run, highlighted by a legendary Dark Star → St. Stephen sequence and crystal‑clear mix.
review: This 16‑track soundboard recording delivers one of the most iconic shows of the late‑60s Dead, with a concise first set and an expansive second set that includes the quintessential Dark Star → St. Stephen, Mountains of the Moon, Dupree’s, Cosmic Charlie and more. Reviewers rave about the crisp separation of Jerry’s guitar, Bob’s rhythm work and Tom’s keyboards, noting it sounds even better than the Live/Dead LP mixes.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of detailed 5‑star reviews praise both performance and audio fidelity; only a single outlier gives 0 stars, resulting in an average near 4.5 and a strong positive rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: 16‑track SBD mix is described as crisp, clear and well‑balanced; guitars and keyboards are highly audible with superb separation. Reviewers note it surpasses the Live/Dead vinyl/CD mixes in fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A brief, three‑song first set followed by a second set that feels purposefully crafted for the historic 16‑track recording; the band plays with heightened focus and cohesion throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is praised as fuzzy to ear‑piercing, with iconic sequences and vocalizations during Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as delivering an impressive “maelstrom” especially in the second set jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar is noted for being clear and creative throughout the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom’s keyboards are highlighted as clearly audible, a rarity on some other mixes.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums receive occasional mention of strong presence (e.g., louder in So Many Roads), but no specific criticism.
